meta.title: Mercedes Vaneo W414 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

hero.h1: Mercedes Vaneo W414 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the Mercedes Vaneo compact MPV — from the M166 1.6-litre petrol to the 1.9-litre petrol and the OM668 1.7 CDI turbodiesel that powered Mercedes' most versatile family carrier. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the sandwich-floor layout that defined this A-Class-based people-mover. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [PATTERN DATA].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes Vaneo W414 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the W414 MPV specifically — including the full M166 petrol range and the OM668 turbodiesel line.

overview.intro: The W414 is the Mercedes-Benz Vaneo, a compact MPV produced from 2001 to 2005 as the brand's first purpose-built people-mover. Based on the first-generation A-Class (W168) platform with its signature 'sandwich floor' construction, the Vaneo offered seating for up to seven passengers in a van-like driving position with a high roofline. The engine range was limited to four-cylinder units — M166 petrols in 1.6-litre and 1.9-litre displacements, plus the OM668 1.7-litre CDI turbodiesel. Front-wheel drive was standard across the range, with manual and automatic transmissions available.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes W168 (first-generation A-Class) • Predecessor: None (first Vaneo) • Successor: Mercedes-Benz B-Class, Citan • Sibling body styles in this generation: Mercedes-Benz A-Class (W168).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The W414 Vaneo generated UK engine enquiries in 2025 [PATTERN DATA], with the M166 petrol and OM668 diesel remaining the most commonly discussed among specialists.

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode — one paragraph: The W414's most dependable engine is the M166.991 1.9-litre petrol (125 PS) — the most powerful option in the range, offering strong performance for the Vaneo's 1.3-tonne kerb weight. The M166.960 1.6-litre petrol (102 PS) is the best value option — simple, robust, and free of turbocharger complexity. The OM668 1.7 CDI diesel offers excellent fuel economy (as low as 5.6 l/100km extra-urban) but suffers from typical common-rail injector and EGR issues as mileage climbs. The M166.961 82 PS petrol is the entry-level engine — underpowered for a seven-seater MPV and best avoided unless you prioritise purchase price over performance. For most buyers, the 1.9-litre petrol represents the sweet spot — enough power for fully loaded family trips without the diesel's DPF and injector complications.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Value — the sensible petrol choice
Entry Choice — underpowered but cheap to run
Most Reliable — the pick of the range
Economy Diesel — excellent MPG, higher maintenance
Best Diesel — more power, same reliability

economicsRule.title: The W414 rule
economicsRule.text: A £1,200–£3,000 recon on a sub-£2,000 W414 sits at 60–150% of the car's value — at that point, scrap value usually wins. Against a well-kept £3,500+ example, the same recon drops to 34–86% — still a serious decision. Our 70% Mercedes-Benz threshold is the line — value the car first, then the engine. Given the Vaneo's low residual values, engine replacement is rarely economically viable unless the car is in exceptional condition.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W414 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 1.6 82 PS (M166.961) — simplest engine, cheapest parts, no turbo.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W414 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 1.7 CDI (OM668.914) — diesel injector and EGR maintenance adds up.

keyTakeaways.question: Which W414 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 1.9 125 PS (M166.991) — best power-to-weight, proven reliability, no diesel complications.

problems.title: M166 Petrol Ignition Coil Failure
problems.rootCause: The M166's coil-on-plug ignition system is prone to coil pack failure, leading to misfires, rough idle, and reduced power. The issue is exacerbated by age and heat cycling in the compact engine bay.

tieredCosts.work: Replace faulty coil pack(s) and spark plugs
Full engine strip and rebuild if misfire has caused bore damage

problems.recommendation: At the first sign of a misfire or rough idle, have the coil packs and spark plugs replaced — £150–£300 is cheap insurance against the £1,200+ replacement that follows if a persistent misfire damages the cylinder bore.

cta.label: Compare M166 engine prices for your W414 Vaneo →

problems.title: OM668 Diesel Injector Wear & EGR Clogging
problems.affectedModels: W414 Vaneo 1.7 CDI (all OM668 diesel variants).
problems.rootCause: The OM668's common-rail injectors wear over time, causing poor cold starting, increased smoke, and reduced fuel economy. The EGR valve clogs with soot, particularly on cars used for short urban journeys.

tieredCosts.work: Full replacement of injectors, EGR, and turbo if damaged

problems.recommendation: Use premium diesel and give the car a regular motorway run to keep the EGR and DPF clear — a £300 injector service every 80,000 miles is far cheaper than the £1,800+ replacement that follows if injector wear leads to bore wash or turbo damage.

cta.label: Compare OM668 engine prices for your W414 Vaneo →

problems.title: Sandwich Floor Engine Mount Wear
problems.rootCause: The Vaneo shares the W168's 'sandwich floor' design, which mounts the engine at an angle behind the front axle. The engine mounts and subframe bushes wear over time, causing clunking, vibration, and misalignment that can accelerate drivetrain wear.

tieredCosts.work: Replace engine mounts and subframe bushes
Full subframe removal and reinforcement if mounts have failed completely

problems.recommendation: Have the engine mounts inspected at every service after 70,000 miles — £300–£500 in preventative work is a fraction of the £900+ cost if worn mounts cause subframe damage or drivetrain misalignment.

cta.label: Compare W414 engine mount replacement quotes →

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are W414 five-door MPV only. The Vaneo shares its platform and engines with the W168 A-Class but is a standalone model. Production ran from 2001 to 2005, with the Vaneo replaced by the B-Class and later the Citan.
